questions.question: Differentiate between semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, and panoptic segmentation. Give one deep learning model example for each type.
keyPoints: Semantic segmentation assigns a class label to each pixel (e.g., FCN, DeepLabV3).
Instance segmentation distinguishes separate objects of the same class (e.g., Mask R-CNN).
Panoptic segmentation combines semantic and instance-level info (e.g., Panoptic FPN).
Models typically use encoder-decoder or feature pyramid structures.

questions.question: Describe the major stages of a deep learning-based face recognition pipeline. Mention one loss function used for learning discriminative face embeddings.
keyPoints: Face detection (e.g., MTCNN, RetinaFace) to locate faces.
Face alignment using detected landmarks to normalize pose.
Feature extraction via a CNN to produce an embedding vector.
Verification/recognition using metric learning (e.g., ArcFace, Triplet loss).

questions.question: Explain the neural style transfer algorithm. Discuss the content loss, style loss, and total variation loss, and describe how a pre‑trained CNN (such as VGG‑19) is used to separate content and style features. Provide a brief pseudocode for implementing style transfer.
keyPoints: Content loss: Euclidean distance between feature representations of generated and content images (usually from a mid‑layer).
Style loss: Match Gram matrices of feature maps from style image and generated image to capture texture.
Total variation loss encourages spatial smoothness in the generated image.
Algorithm: initialize generated image, extract features with VGG‑19, compute weighted sum of losses, update generated image via gradient descent.

questions.question: Compare the architectural innovations of ResNet, ResNeXt, and EfficientNet. Discuss how they address vanishing gradients and computational efficiency. Provide a short PyTorch code snippet that loads a pre‑trained EfficientNet‑B0 and fine‑tunes it for a custom image classification task.
keyPoints: ResNet uses skip connections (residual blocks) to enable gradient flow and mitigate vanishing gradients.
ResNeXt adds cardinality (grouped convolutions) to increase representational capacity while keeping computational budget similar.
EfficientNet applies compound scaling (depth, width, resolution) to achieve better accuracy and efficiency.

PyTorch snippet: model = torchvision.models.efficientnet_b0(pretrained=True); model.classifier = nn.Linear(1280, num_classes); optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=1e-4); training loop.
